original text:
First, install the dependencies. This for Ubuntu Server 14.04.
Create new file (e.g. launch-spot.py) and give exec permissions (chmod launch-spot.py).
The script less than 200 lines long and should readable from top bottom.
Set your Amazon AWS access keys environment variables.
you can read the boto documentation find out how store the keys configuration files.
Waiting for launch
Waiting for instance boot
stands for --name and how you can identify this spot instance.
The profiles are hard coded the script. Feel free modify the script load them from external configuration file (in JSON perhaps).
You can run the script again and long you use the same name will resume.
run script this instance via SSH use one more arguments.
Connecting SSH [54.74.149.116]
You can copy paste the last line your terminal and connect the instance.
you can see, you haven't specified key_pair the profile, new key pair will created with the name KP-test and will saved KP-test.pem.
there's security_group the profile, new one will created with the name SG-test and the rules from the firewall profile setting will applied. minimum, SSH from your should allowed.
you want stop the spot instance, simply run sudo halt the instance. The instance, the instance request and disks (if auto terminate on) will automatically shut down and terminated.
you use simple bash script, suggest using one like this. set will stop the execution error occurs. the script finished successfully, will not run again.
you share provisioning script with Vagrant and AWS, you can use this line check you are running AWS EC2 locally Vagrant.
Amazon Ubuntu mirrors and apt-get can slow and flaky. Especially you hit CTRL+C while the script executing apt-get and then run again.
Create file system the instance disk and move /home, /tmp and /swapfile it. This can useful large SSD backed instance disks.
upload/download files AWS and use local files /vagrant when running Vagrant, you could something like this.

Create new file (e.g. launch-spot.py) and give exec permissions (chmod launch-spot.py).
The script less than 200 lines long and should readable from top bottom.
Set your Amazon AWS access keys environment variables.
you can read the boto documentation find out how store the keys configuration files.
Waiting for launch
Waiting for instance boot
stands for --name and how you can identify this spot instance.
The profiles are hard coded the script. Feel free modify the script load them from external configuration file (in JSON perhaps).
You can run the script again and long you use the same name will resume.
run script this instance via SSH use one more arguments.
Connecting SSH []
You can copy paste the last line your terminal and connect the instance.
you can see, you haven't specified key_pair the profile, new key pair will created with the name KP-test and will saved KP-test.pem.
there's security_group the profile, new one will created with the name SG-test and the rules from the firewall profile setting will applied. minimum, SSH from your should allowed.
you want stop the spot instance, simply run sudo halt the instance. The instance, the instance request and disks (if auto terminate on) will automatically shut down and terminated.
you use simple bash script, suggest using one like this. set will stop the execution error occurs. the script finished successfully, will not run again.
you share provisioning script with Vagrant and AWS, you can use this line check you are running AWS EC2 locally Vagrant.
Amazon Ubuntu mirrors and apt-get can slow and flaky. Especially you hit CTRL+C while the script executing apt-get and then run again.
Create file system the instance disk and move /home, /tmp and /swapfile it. This can useful large SSD backed instance disks.
upload/download files AWS and use local files /vagrant when running Vagrant, you could something like this.
